# Positive Constraint

> Positive Constraint is the practice of Omer Yarkowich — finding the core constraint at the center of a problem, abstracting away everything non-essential, and turning limits into the shape of the solution. These pages are ideas meant to be read, quoted, and carried into your own thinking. Each idea page has a "Copy as markdown" and "Download .md" button so you can bring it into an AI conversation cleanly.

## Core ideas

- [Positive Constraint](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/positive-constraint/): A constraint that shapes progress rather than blocking it — the central idea the whole practice is named for.
- [Core Constraints](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/core-constraints/): The irreducible truth at the center of a problem domain — what remains after you abstract away everything non-essential.
- [Abstraction](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/abstraction/): Filtering out unnecessary detail to surface what truly matters — the core cognitive tool behind everything here.
- [Innovation](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/innovation/): New solutions to pre-existing problems given new constraints — a changed constraint is what makes a new solution possible.
- [The Three Axes](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/three-axes/): Every company is defined by three axes — one northstar KPI plus the 2×2 worldview it wants the market to adopt.
- [Desired Reality](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/desired-reality/): The goal expressed in the language of Positive Constraint — your Now reframed as the reality you want, so the mind conjures the ways to reach it.
- [The Process](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/process/): The four phases every engagement follows, and how their depth scales with the project.
- [3 Reds in Haifa](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/three-reds-in-haifa/): A reflection — three red lights, two kids, and a dental bill — on how a red light is a constraint too: an unchanging element that shapes everything around it.
- [Embrace the Unchanging](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/embrace-the-unchanging/): The world is too complex to predict and too fast to adapt to. The path to resilience lies not in what's changing, but in what isn't — and finding it is something only humans can do.
- [Complex New World](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/complex-new-world/): The world has become a polycrisis — too complex to predict and too fast to adapt to. Why out-changing your environment no longer works, and what has to replace it.
- [The Constraint Behind Prince of Persia's Best Fight](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/prince-of-persia-shadow-man/): A memory limit left Jordan Mechner no room for a new boss — so he flipped the Prince into his own reflection. The constraint made the fight better.

## Practice & work

- [Services](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/services/): The three services, all drawn from the same foundation of core constraints and abstraction.
- [Work](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/work/): How value creation shows up across industries, geographies, and technologies.
- [CortiSense](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/work-cortisense/): Predictive maintenance / IoT, Israel — value proposition and B2B pitch.
- [LEAP Commerce](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/work-leap-commerce/): eCommerce enablement, Singapore — investment pitch and value strategy.
- [Tapouts](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/work-tapouts/): Emotional well-being platform for kids, USA — pitch deck and early-stage strategy.
- [User1st](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/work-user1st/): Developer tools for inclusion, Israel — value strategy and investor pitch.

## Optional

- [About](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/about/): Who Omer is and why he founded Positive Constraint.
- [FAQ](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/faq/): Common questions about working together.
- [Contact](https://positiveconstraint.com/ideas/contact/): How to get in touch.
- [Knowledge map](https://positiveconstraint.com/map/): An interactive map of how all the ideas connect.
